textInfo.title: Prostate Tissue Appearance Changes
textInfo.medicalDescription:
Mittelgradige Heterogenität
der peripheren Zone in der T2-gewichteten Sequenz. Interpretation: Gering BPH
Veränderungen
textInfo.originalDescription:
Mittelgradige Heterogenität
der peripheren Zone in der T2-gewichteten Sequenz. Interpretation: Gering BPH
Veränderungen
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: Your MRI shows that the tissue in the outer area of your prostate, known as the peripheral zone, has a moderately varied or non-uniform appearance. This finding is consistent with mild changes of Benign Prostatic Hyperplasia (BPH). BPH refers to a common, non-cancerous enlargement and change in texture of the prostate gland that occurs with age.

textInfo.title: Slightly Low White Blood Cell Count
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: Your white blood cell (leukocyte) count is 3.56 10E9/l, which is slightly below the normal range of 3.90-10.20 10E9/l. White blood cells are key to your immune system. A slightly low count can be temporary and may be caused by various factors, including viral infections or certain medications. As it's only slightly low, it often doesn't indicate a serious problem, but monitoring it over time may be suggested.

textInfo.title: Anatomical Variation of Ophthalmic Arteries in the Brain
textInfo.medicalDescription:
Symmetrische Gefäßaussackung am Abgang der Arteria ophthalmica
bds. (Serie 12, Image 99 und 102), am ehesten infundibulärer Abgang
des Gefäßes.
textInfo.originalDescription:
Symmetrische Gefäßaussackung am Abgang der Arteria ophthalmica
bds. (Serie 12, Image 99 und 102), am ehesten infundibulärer Abgang
des Gefäßes.
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: Your brain scan shows a symmetrical, funnel-shaped widening of a blood vessel on both the left and right sides, specifically where the ophthalmic artery originates. This is most likely an 'infundibular origin,' which is a common and normal anatomical variation in the way the blood vessel is shaped at its branching point. This is not considered a true aneurysm but rather a feature of your individual anatomy.

textInfo.title: Mild Disc Wear and Spinal Narrowing in Your Neck
textInfo.medicalDescription:
Diskopathie
HWK 5/HWK 6 und HWK 6/HWK 7 mit jeweils leichtgradiger spinaler Enge
textInfo.originalDescription:
Diskopathie
HWK 5/HWK 6 und HWK 6/HWK 7 mit jeweils leichtgradiger spinaler Enge
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: Your MRI shows signs of disc wear, a process known as discopathy, at two levels in your neck: between the 5th and 6th cervical vertebrae (C5/C6) and between the 6th and 7th (C6/C7). This condition involves the gradual breakdown of the discs, which act as cushions between your vertebrae. Associated with this, there is a mild narrowing of the spinal canal at these levels. The spinal canal is the central channel that houses your spinal cord.

textInfo.title: Total Brain Volume
textInfo.medicalDescription: The patient's total brain volume percentile is 2.3%, which is low compared to a normal population of the same age and sex.
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: Your total brain volume percentile is 2.3%. This means your brain volume is smaller than approximately 97.7% of individuals of the same age and sex in a reference population. This is a statistical comparison and its clinical significance depends on other factors and your overall health.

textInfo.title: Low Ferritin Level
textInfo.medicalDescription: 15-30 µg/l: leere oder knappe Eisenspeicher. (15-30 µg/l: empty or low iron stores).
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: Your ferritin level is 21.7 µg/l, which is below the standard reference range of 30-350 µg/l. Ferritin is a protein that stores iron in your body. This low level indicates that your body's iron stores are depleted, which can be a precursor to iron-deficiency anemia.

textInfo.title: Minor Disc Wear in Your Mid-Back
textInfo.medicalDescription:
Geringe Diskopathie BWK 11/BWK 12 ohne spinale
oder neuroforaminale Enge
textInfo.originalDescription:
Geringe Diskopathie BWK 11/BWK 12 ohne spinale
oder neuroforaminale Enge
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: The scan of your thoracic spine, or mid-back, shows minor disc wear (discopathy) between the 11th and 12th thoracic vertebrae. Importantly, the report specifies that this wear has not caused any narrowing of the main spinal canal or the openings where nerves exit the spine (neuroforamina). This means that despite the minor age-related changes to the disc, there is no pressure being placed on your spinal cord or nerve roots at this level.

textInfo.title: Reduced Total Thigh Muscle Volume
textInfo.medicalDescription: The total fat-free muscle volume of the thighs is 8.02 L, which falls below the normative range of 9.68 - 16.0 L. This finding is indicative of generalized reduced muscle mass in the thighs (sarcopenia).
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: The total volume of fat-free muscle in both your thighs is 8.02 L, which is below the expected range of 9.68 L to 16.0 L. This indicates a general reduction in thigh muscle mass, which is a key factor for overall strength, mobility, and metabolic health.

textInfo.title: Left Parietal Lobe Volume
textInfo.medicalDescription: The patient's left parietal lobe volume percentile is 2.7%, which is low compared to a normal population of the same age and sex.
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: The volume of your left parietal lobe is at the 2.7th percentile. This means its volume is smaller than approximately 97.3% of individuals of the same age and sex in a reference population. The parietal lobe is involved in processing sensory information, and the clinical relevance of this finding depends on your overall neurological health and any related symptoms.

textInfo.title: Increased Fat within Left Anterior Thigh Muscle
textInfo.medicalDescription: Muscle fat infiltration (MFI) in the anterior compartment of the left thigh is elevated at 8.05%, exceeding the normal range of 4.18 - 7.94%. This indicates a degree of myosteatosis.
textInfo.originalDescription: biomarker_name: MFI thighs AT_left, Value: 8.05325, Range: 4.18 - 7.94
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: The amount of fat within the muscles in the front of your left thigh is 8.05%, which is slightly above the typical range of 4.18% to 7.94%. This condition, known as muscle fat infiltration, can affect the quality and function of the muscle.

textInfo.title: Elevated Fat Infiltration in Right Psoas Muscle
textInfo.medicalDescription: The intramuscular fat (IMF) of the right psoas major muscle is 9.0%, which is above the normal range of 4.7% to 7.7%.
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: The amount of fat within your right psoas muscle, a key core muscle, is elevated above the typical range. Higher levels of intramuscular fat can be associated with reduced muscle quality and function, and may indicate a need to focus on core strengthening and overall physical activity.

textInfo.title: Elevated Fat Infiltration in Left Psoas Muscle
textInfo.medicalDescription: The intramuscular fat (IMF) of the left psoas major muscle is 8.6%, which is above the normal range of 5.3% to 8.3%.
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: The amount of fat within your left psoas muscle, a key core muscle, is elevated above the typical range. Higher levels of intramuscular fat can be associated with reduced muscle quality and function, and may indicate a need to focus on core strengthening and overall physical activity.

textInfo.title: Increased Fat within Right Anterior Thigh Muscle
textInfo.medicalDescription: Muscle fat infiltration (MFI) in the anterior compartment of the right thigh is slightly elevated at 7.89%, exceeding the normal range of 3.9 - 7.74%. This indicates a degree of myosteatosis.
textInfo.originalDescription: biomarker_name: MFI thighs AT_right, Value: 7.8882, Range: 3.9 - 7.74
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: The amount of fat within the muscles in the front of your right thigh is 7.89%, which is slightly above the typical range of 3.9% to 7.74%. This condition, known as muscle fat infiltration, can affect the quality and function of the muscle.

textInfo.title: Reduced Muscle Volume in Left Posterior Thigh
textInfo.medicalDescription: The fat-free muscle volume of the posterior compartment of the left thigh is 2.62 L, which falls below the normative range of 3.15 - 5.19 L. This finding is indicative of reduced muscle mass in this region.
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: The volume of fat-free muscle in the back part of your left thigh is 2.62 L, which is below the expected range of 3.15 L to 5.19 L. Lower muscle volume can be a sign of muscle loss (atrophy) and may impact strength and mobility. Maintaining muscle mass is important for overall health.

textInfo.title: Minor Inflammatory Change in Your Lower Spine
textInfo.medicalDescription: Gering aktivierte Veränderung der Endplatten LWK 5/SWK 1.
textInfo.originalDescription: Gering aktivierte Veränderung der Endplatten LWK 5/SWK 1.
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: The MRI of your lower back shows a minor 'activated change' in the endplates at the junction between your last lumbar vertebra (L5) and your sacrum (S1). Endplates are the top and bottom surfaces of the vertebrae that connect to the spinal discs. An 'activated change' suggests a small, localized area of inflammation or fluid in the bone marrow adjacent to the disc. This is often a reaction to the stress and degenerative changes occurring in the disc itself and is a common finding.

textInfo.title: High LDL Cholesterol
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: Your LDL ('bad') cholesterol level is 3.9 mmol/l, which is above the recommended level of less than 3.0 mmol/l. High LDL cholesterol is a major risk factor for heart disease, as it can lead to plaque buildup in your arteries (atherosclerosis).

textInfo.title: Slightly Low Mean Corpuscular Hemoglobin Concentration (MCHC)
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: Your MCHC level is 314 g/l, which is slightly below the normal range of 315-365 g/l. MCHC measures the average concentration of hemoglobin inside your red blood cells. A slightly low value can sometimes be seen in certain types of anemia, but given your other blood counts are mostly normal, this isolated finding is often not clinically significant.

textInfo.title: Reduced Muscle Volume in Right Posterior Thigh
textInfo.medicalDescription: The fat-free muscle volume of the posterior compartment of the right thigh is 2.48 L, which falls below the normative range of 3.18 - 5.26 L. This finding is indicative of reduced muscle mass in this region.
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: The volume of fat-free muscle in the back part of your right thigh is 2.48 L, which is below the expected range of 3.18 L to 5.26 L. Lower muscle volume can be a sign of muscle loss (atrophy) and may impact strength and mobility. Maintaining muscle mass is important for overall health.

textInfo.title: Reduced Muscle Volume in Left Anterior Thigh
textInfo.medicalDescription: The fat-free muscle volume of the anterior compartment of the left thigh is 1.32 L, which falls below the normative range of 1.54 - 2.86 L. This finding is indicative of reduced muscle mass in this region.
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: The volume of fat-free muscle in the front part of your left thigh is 1.32 L, which is below the expected range of 1.54 L to 2.86 L. Lower muscle volume can be a sign of muscle loss (atrophy) and may impact strength and mobility. Maintaining muscle mass is important for overall health.

textInfo.title: Increased Total Fat within Thigh Muscles
textInfo.medicalDescription: The total muscle fat infiltration (MFI) of the thighs is elevated at 8.51%, exceeding the normal range of 4.07 - 7.79%. This indicates a degree of generalized myosteatosis in the thighs.
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: The total amount of fat within your thigh muscles is 8.51%, which is above the typical range of 4.07% to 7.79%. This condition, known as muscle fat infiltration, affects overall muscle quality and can impact strength and metabolic function.

textInfo.title: Prostate Volume Measurement
textInfo.medicalDescription: Prostatavolumen: 25 ml (Normwert: <30 ml).
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: Your prostate has been measured to have a volume of 25 milliliters (ml). This measurement is within the typical normal range, which is generally considered to be less than 30 ml for an adult male. Therefore, your prostate size is not enlarged based on this imaging.

textInfo.title: Anatomical Variation of the Pancreatic Duct
textInfo.medicalDescription: Mündung des Ductus pancreaticus in die Papilla duodeni minor
textInfo.originalDescription: Mündung des Ductus pancreaticus in die Papilla duodeni minor
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: Your scan shows an anatomical variation in your pancreas where the main pancreatic duct drains into a structure called the 'papilla duodeni minor'. Typically, this duct drains into a different opening. This finding simply describes the specific way your pancreatic duct is structured, which is a normal variation of human anatomy and not a sign of disease.

textInfo.title: Simple Kidney Cyst on the Left
textInfo.medicalDescription:
Einfache
kortikale Nierenzyste am Oberpol links mit 10 mm
textInfo.originalDescription:
Einfache
kortikale Nierenzyste am Oberpol links mit 10 mm
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: The MRI shows a simple cyst on your left kidney, measuring 10 millimeters. This cyst is located in the cortex, which is the outer layer of the kidney, specifically at the top portion (upper pole). A simple cyst is a benign, fluid-filled sac with a thin wall, much like a blister. These are very common, especially with age, and are generally not a cause for concern.

textInfo.title: High Non-HDL Cholesterol
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: Your Non-HDL cholesterol level is 4.2 mmol/l, which is above the recommended level of less than 3.9 mmol/l. Non-HDL cholesterol includes all the 'bad' types of cholesterol and is considered a good indicator of cardiovascular risk. Higher levels can contribute to the buildup of plaque in arteries.

textInfo.title: High Total Cholesterol
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: Your total cholesterol level is 5.8 mmol/l, which is above the recommended level of less than 5 mmol/l. High total cholesterol is a risk factor for cardiovascular disease. Managing it often involves lifestyle changes like diet and exercise, and sometimes medication.

textInfo.medicalDescription: Colon sigmoideum
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: The MRI visualized a section of your large intestine known as the sigmoid colon, where a mild, uncomplicated diverticulosis was noted. This refers to small pouches in the colon wall that are common with age and, in your case, are without signs of inflammation or other complications. No further abnormalities were observed in this region.
textInfo.title: Diverticulosis Sigmoid Colon
Disc Wear in Lower Spine with Possible Nerve Root Proximity
textInfo.medicalDescription:
Diskopathie LWK 5/SWK 1. Keine spinale
Enge. Keine neuroforaminale Enge. Mögliche rezessale Enge rechts (Serie
49, Image 86) mit möglicher Affektion der S1 Wurzel rechts.
textInfo.originalDescription:
Diskopathie LWK 5/SWK 1. Keine spinale
Enge. Keine neuroforaminale Enge. Mögliche rezessale Enge rechts (Serie
49, Image 86) mit möglicher Affektion der S1 Wurzel rechts.
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: Your MRI shows disc wear (discopathy) at the lowest level of your lumbar spine, between the 5th lumbar vertebra (L5) and the top of your sacrum (S1). The report confirms there is no narrowing of the main central spinal canal or of the primary openings (neuroforamina) where nerves exit. However, it notes a 'possible' narrowing on the right side in a small channel called the lateral recess. This narrowing could potentially affect the S1 nerve root on the right side. The term 'possible' indicates that the nerve root is close to the area of narrowing, but it is not definitively being compressed.

textInfo.title: Simple Liver Cyst
textInfo.medicalDescription: Einfache Zyste von 4 mm im Segment römisch 8
textInfo.originalDescription: Einfache Zyste von 4 mm im Segment römisch 8
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: Your MRI scan revealed a small, simple cyst in your liver, measuring 4 millimeters in diameter. A simple cyst is a benign, thin-walled sac filled with fluid, similar to a small blister. This finding is located in segment 8 of the liver, which is in the upper right portion. Such cysts are very common and are not considered cancerous or a cause for concern.

textInfo.title: Complex Kidney Cyst on the Right
textInfo.medicalDescription:
T2
hypointense, T1 hyperintense Läsion von 8 mm am Unterpol rechts (Serie
71, Image 90), keine Diffusionsrestriktion. Interpretation: Am ehesten proteinreiche/eingeblutete
Nierenzyste am Unterpol rechts.
textInfo.originalDescription:
T2
hypointense, T1 hyperintense Läsion von 8 mm am Unterpol rechts (Serie
71, Image 90), keine Diffusionsrestriktion. Interpretation: Am ehesten proteinreiche/eingeblutete
Nierenzyste am Unterpol rechts.
textInfo.detailedLaymanDescription: Your MRI identified an 8 mm lesion at the bottom part (lower pole) of your right kidney. This lesion has specific characteristics on different MRI sequences (appearing dark on T2 images and bright on T1 images), which suggests it contains substances other than simple fluid. This is most likely a benign kidney cyst that contains protein-rich fluid or has had minor bleeding into it in the past. The report also notes there is 'no diffusion restriction,' which is a reassuring sign that makes a malignant tumor less likely.
